body.title: mcp__igor__request_compaction claims 'will fire on next turn' but compact does not execute
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Observed 2026-04-24 ~11:10 CDT: invoked mcp__igor__request_compaction with a fresh preserve string. Tool returned 'Compact queued via file handoff. Will fire on next turn.' and wrote ~/.TheIgors/cc_compact_pending.txt. On the next user turn, the file was gone (consumed by the cc_hook_pending.py UserPromptSubmit hook), but /compact did NOT execute — the conversation continued at full context.

Secondary symptom all day 2026-04-24: the UserPromptSubmit hook kept re-injecting a stale compact request with `preserve: session=2026-04-16a` across multiple turns, even without new request_compaction calls from me. That stale string is from a different session and keeps surfacing — suggesting either (a) the COMPACT_PENDING_FILE was getting re-written by some automated path using a fixed template, or (b) there are multiple compact-request sources and one holds stale state that never drains.

Root causes to investigate:
1. **File-handoff mechanism does not execute /compact** — cc_hook_pending.py:194-201 only injects a `--- COMPACT REQUESTED ---` additionalContext message describing the preserve string. It does NOT run `/compact` itself. Claude Code's harness does not auto-invoke /compact on seeing the injected suggestion. Either (a) the injection should also fire a tmux send-keys fallback that literally types /compact into the session, OR (b) the hook should be an explicit directive Claude Code's SDK recognizes as 'run /compact now'.
2. **Stale-preserve leak source** — the session=2026-04-16a preserve kept resurfacing across turns. COMPACT_PENDING_FILE.unlink is called on each read, so there must be another writer. Candidates: the /loop skill's ScheduleWakeup path (CronCreate schedule persistence), lingering MCP server state, or a hook that re-injects from a cached location.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_hook_pending.py (the reader), lab/claudecode/igor_mcp.py:905-945 (request_compaction tool, already has a tmux fallback path — figure out why it isn't being taken), tests/test_compact_file_handoff.py (existing test — verify it covers the fire-through, or add that case). **Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not. **Scope boundary:** IN — make mcp__igor__request_compaction actually cause /compact to execute; fix stale-preserve leak source. OUT — redesigning the compact mechanism end-to-end, changing Claude Code SDK behavior (that's upstream). **Test plan:** (1) invoke request_compaction, assert the pending file is written. (2) simulate next-turn hook invocation with mocked stdin; assert the hook's output triggers /compact execution (or note that the hook CAN'T trigger it and we need a different mechanism). (3) Reproduce the stale-preserve leak: write preserve=X, consume via hook (file deleted), verify NO further hook turn re-injects 'X'.
